title: Security+
markdown: ## My Journey with CompTIA Security+ SY0-701: Reflections and Insights Hey everyone! 👋 I wanted to share my experience preparing for and passing the **CompTIA Security+ SY0-701** certification exam. This was my first CompTIA certification, and I found the process both challenging and rewarding. Before this, I had earned certifications like AWS Solutions Architect and Linux Foundation Certified Kubernetes Architect, but Security+ was my introduction to core cybersecurity concepts. *** ### Why I Chose Security+ Although I hadn’t yet worked directly in cybersecurity or threat analysis, my previous roles involved improving product security, fixing vulnerabilities, and implementing least privilege principles. More recently, I got involved in a threat intelligence project analyzing network data to detect potential threats. Here's your CompTIA Security+ experience rewritten with different wording and tone, avoiding any mention of INE or specific providers: *** ## My Experience Preparing for and Passing CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Hello everyone! 👋 I wanted to take a moment to share my journey with the **CompTIA Security+ SY0-701** exam, which I recently took and passed. This was my very first CompTIA certification, and it marked a significant milestone in my IT and cybersecurity learning path. Prior to this, I had earned certifications like AWS Solutions Architect and Linux Foundation Certified Kubernetes Architect, but Security+ introduced me to foundational cybersecurity knowledge. *** #### Why I Decided to Pursue Security+ My initial exposure to cybersecurity was somewhat indirect — I worked on improving product security, patching vulnerabilities, applying least privilege policies, and following well-architected frameworks. More recently, I had the chance to contribute to a flagship threat intelligence tool that analyzed network traffic for potential cyber threats. That experience opened my eyes to the fascinating world of cybersecurity, and motivated me to dive deeper by pursuing a certification. *** #### How I Prepared For study materials, I found a popular course on Udemy that covered the SY0-701 syllabus extensively, including practice exams. I spent about a month preparing, dedicating roughly two hours a day to watching video lectures and reviewing study notes. The course was comprehensive but digestible — it covered many topics without being overwhelming, which fit well for a foundational certification. Some of the key areas I focused on included different encryption methods (symmetric and asymmetric), hashing, tokenization, port security protocols like 802.1x and EAP, attack types, and incident response processes. Surprisingly, the course also covered physical security topics like RFID and NFC access controls, which, while unexpected, made sense given the certification’s broad scope. *** #### Taking the Exam The exam itself was delivered online through a proctored platform, which I appreciated for its convenience. The questions were tricky at times — simple wording, but scenarios often had multiple plausible answers. I made good use of the option to flag questions for review, which helped me double-check tricky ones before submitting. There were also a couple of practical questions, such as configuring a VPN tunnel and analyzing a network for infected devices. Although I couldn’t specifically prepare for those, I felt ready thanks to the broad coverage of the course. When I finished, I was thrilled to see an immediate passing score of 790 out of 900 — no long waits or nail-biting! *** #### Key Takeaways and Tips Overall, I’m very happy with the knowledge gained and feel more confident tackling cybersecurity challenges going forward. If you’re planning to take this exam, here are a few pointers from my experience: * Consider watching multiple instructors’ videos to find the style that suits you best. * The exam usually has a few practical questions upfront — you might want to flag those and come back later. * If English isn’t your first language, requesting extra time on the exam can be very helpful. * Dedicate consistent daily time to studying, and use practice exams to identify areas to improve. *** Thanks for reading! If you’re on your own certification journey, I hope this helps you navigate the process. Good luck out there! ✌️ *** ### 📚 Study & Preparation Roadmap Here is my recommended study flow for the **CompTIA Security+**: 1. **Theory & Concepts**: - Watch **Professor Messer's Security+** free video series on YouTube. It is one of the highest-rated resources. - Study security principles, threat vectors, risk management, and cryptography basics. 2. **Practice Exams**: - Use mock exams (like Jason Dion's or Sybex) to get used to the wording and format of CompTIA questions. 3. **Performance-Based Questions (PBQs)**: - Prepare for firewall configurations, network architecture diagram matches, and matching security controls. 4. **Exam Day Tips**: - Flag and skip the PBQs at the start; complete all multiple-choice questions first, then return to the PBQs with your remaining time. - Read the questions carefully, paying close attention to keywords like "BEST", "MOST", or "FIRST".
